mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate trajectories are observable.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of June 2018 reflects accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant outperformance versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of June 2018 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC of the trigger signal is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth exceeding their 12-month median simultaneously, aggregate demand expansion drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month median growth rate in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971M and HOUST at 1350K as of May 2018
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST growth) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC of the decline signal is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth exceeds 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE is below 4.2%, the labor market is overheating, signaling inflation pressure and future Fed tightening that produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148912K and UNRATE at 4.0% as of June 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, creating negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.857 and FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of June 2018
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the real rate spread signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC of the growth deceleration signal is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth exceeds 180K, jointly signaling robust supply expansion and labor productivity gains that support margin expansion and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS growth > 180K in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3 and PAYEMS at 148912K as of May-June 2018
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized supply and labor growth
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term (INDPRO growth × PAYEMS growth) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
